hero.title: Your finances,
hero.subtitle: Track, analyze, and optimize your money in one place.
hero.description: Track income and expenses, control budgets by category, and understand your financial habits with clear visual reports.
hero.feature1: Visualize your income and expenses in real time
hero.feature3: Your data secured with Supabase Auth

features.transactionsDesc: Record income and expenses with categories, description, and date. Filter by type or category and paginate through your entire history.
features.budgetsDesc: Assign a monthly limit to each category. FinTrack automatically calculates how much you've spent and alerts you when you're near the limit.
features.reportsDesc: Visualize the evolution of your income and expenses month by month, identify your top spending categories, and export data to CSV.

allFeatures.title: Everything included, no complications
allFeatures.subtitle: Every feature was designed to make taking control of your finances simple and straightforward.
allFeatures.d1: Record income and expenses with category, date, and description.
allFeatures.d2: Monthly limits by category with automatic tracking.
allFeatures.d3: Trend and distribution charts exportable to CSV.
allFeatures.d4: Secure authentication. Only you see your data.

cta.title: Start controlling your finances today
cta.subtitle: Create your account for free and start recording your transactions in under a minute.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.message: The address you're looking for doesn't exist or was moved.

hero.desc: A fullstack personal finance management application. This page documents its architecture, tech stack, design patterns, and implementation decisions.

sidebar.ctaDesc: Create a free account and explore the live app.

stackSection.title: Technologies used
stackSection.lead: FinTrack is a modern fullstack application. The frontend consumes the backend REST API which connects to Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Storage) as infrastructure.

react.desc: Functional components, custom hooks, reusable UI composition.

vite.desc: Instant HMR in development. Bundling with manual code-splitting for optimized loading.

tanstack.desc: Automatic cache, background refetch, queryKey-based invalidation. No Redux.

zustand.desc: Minimal store for session and user profile. No boilerplate, no manual Context API.

rhf.desc: Typed client-side validation. Reusable Zod schemas integrated with resolvers.

framer.desc: Page transitions, scroll animations, floating elements. Declarative and performant.

tailwind.desc: Utility-first with automatic dark mode. Mobile-first responsive design throughout.

vitest.desc: 28 unit tests. vi.mock() for Supabase, vi.setSystemTime() for deterministic dates.

dotnet.desc: Top-tier performance, strong typing, cross-platform. API compiles to native executable.

aspnet.desc: Attribute-based routing, configurable middleware, Model Binding, automatic validation.

efcore.desc: Typed LINQ queries, automatic migrations, InMemory provider for integration tests.

automapper.desc: Clean separation between DB entities and API contracts. Centralized mapping profiles.

xunit.desc: 12 integration tests with EF InMemory. Readable assertions with .Should().Be().

supabase.desc: Managed database, ready-to-use JWT authentication, and file storage with RLS policies.

vercel.desc: Automatic deploy on push to main. SPA rewrites configured in vercel.json for React Router.

render.desc: .NET 9 API hosting. Free tier with cold start. Deploy from Git repository.

archSection.title: System architecture
archSection.lead: The system follows a three-layer architecture: React client, .NET REST API, and Supabase as backend-as-a-service. Files go directly to Storage without going through the API, reducing latency and server load.
archSection.flow1d: The user authenticates with Supabase. An HS256 JWT signed with the project secret is returned.
archSection.flow2d: The frontend attaches the JWT in the Authorization header. The API middleware validates the signature and extracts the user_id.
archSection.flow3d: EF Core translates LINQ to SQL and queries PostgreSQL. RLS policies guarantee per-user isolation.
archSection.flow4d: Receipts are uploaded directly from the browser to Supabase Storage (bypassing the API) using the same JWT.

featSection.title: Screens and functionality
featSection.lead: Five main modules plus an admin panel. Each module has its own page, data hook, and set of components.
dashFeatures: Balance, income and expense KPIs for current month
txFeatures: Filters by type, category and date range
budgetFeatures: Monthly limit per category, UNIQUE in DB
savingsFeatures: Monthly goals with custom name, icon and color
Progress bar with percentage calculated in API
reportsFeatures: Custom SVG bar chart (no external library)
CSV export of full month history

apiSection.title: Backend endpoints
apiSection.lead: REST API built with ASP.NET Core. All endpoints require JWT Bearer unless otherwise noted. The user_id is extracted from the token, never accepted as a client parameter.

desc.txGet: Paginated list. Filters: type, category, date range, text search.
desc.txPost: Create transaction. Supports receipt_url for voucher in Supabase Storage.
desc.txPut: Update. Only the owner can modify their transaction.
desc.txDelete: Delete. Service verifies user_id before deleting.
desc.catGet: Returns the user's own categories plus system categories (is_system = true).
desc.catPost: Create custom category with name, icon and hex color.
desc.catDelete: Delete own category. System categories cannot be deleted.
desc.budGet: Budgets for the month with real-time spent percentage.
desc.budPost: Create monthly limit per category. Unique per (user, category, month).
desc.savGet: Monthly goals with progress and completion percentage.
desc.savPost: Create goal with name, icon, color and target amount.
desc.savPut: Update current amount or any field.
desc.repGet: Monthly summary: totals by type, top categories, 6-month trend.
desc.admGet: Global system statistics (admin role only). Total users, transaction volume.

dbSection.title: Database schema
dbSection.lead: PostgreSQL managed by Supabase. Five tables with foreign keys and Row Level Security on all of them. Users can only read and modify their own data.

tableDesc.profiles: Extends Supabase auth.users. Automatically created on registration.
tableDesc.categories: Transaction categorization. Can be system-wide (visible to all) or user-specific.
tableDesc.transactions: Central register of financial movements. Each row belongs to a single user.
tableDesc.budgets: Monthly spending limits per category. UNIQUE constraint prevents duplicates.
tableDesc.savings_goals: Monthly savings goals with progress tracking.

secSection.title: Authentication and security
secSection.lead: Multiple security layers: JWT in the API, Row Level Security in the database, and Storage policies in Supabase. The backend never trusts the client_id from the request body.

jwt.title: JWT HS256
jwt.desc: Supabase issues tokens signed with the project secret. The .NET middleware validates signature, expiration and claims without calling Supabase on every request.

rls.title: Row Level Security
rls.desc: RLS policies on all tables. WHERE user_id = auth.uid() guarantees each user only accesses their own records, even with direct SQL.

roles.title: User roles
roles.desc: Two roles: "user" and "admin". Admins bypass RLS for global statistics. AdminRoute on the frontend blocks the route if the profile doesn't have the admin role.

storage.title: Storage policies
storage.desc: The "receipts" bucket policies restrict INSERT, SELECT and DELETE to the user's own folder (receipts/{userId}/*). No server-side, directly from browser.

testsSection.title: Test suite
testsSection.lead: 40 tests total — 28 unit tests on the frontend (Vitest) and 12 integration tests on the backend (xUnit). Each backend test gets its own in-memory database for total isolation.
testsSection.utilsDesc: formatCurrency, formatDate, formatRelativeDate (with setSystemTime), monthLabel, toYearMonth, getAvatarGradient
testsSection.receiptDesc: Validates MIME type and size. Supabase Storage mock with vi.mock(). Verifies remove() path.

pagination.name: Pagination (3)
pagination.desc: No filters returns all · offset pagination · no data from other user

filters.name: Filters (3)
filters.desc: Filter by type · case-insensitive search · date range filter

totals.name: Totals (2)
totals.desc: TotalIncome and TotalExpense calculated · totals respect active type filter

crud.name: CRUD (4)
crud.desc: CreateAsync saves to DB · DeleteAsync removes · returns false for non-existent ID · does not delete others' data

deploySection.title: Infrastructure and deployment
deploySection.lead: Three independent cloud services, all on free tier. The frontend deploys automatically on push to main. The backend requires manual deploy or CI/CD configured on Render.

items: Free tier with ~30 s cold start
